Doha, Qatar: Minister of Interior and Commander of the Internal Security Force (Lekhwiya) HE Sheikh Khalifa bin Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani met with the Qatari security force set to participate in securing the Paris 2024 Olympic Games.The Minister encouraged the force, comprised of leaders, experts, and security specialists, along with members of specialized teams and units, to utilize their skills and extensive experience to ensure the successful security of the event. He emphasized that Qatari national cadres have gained global recognition, especially following Qatar's outstanding success in hosting various international events to the highest security standards.He conveyed greetings and directives from the Amir HH Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, and his directives that their mission in France is an extension of their responsibilities in Qatar, therefore, they have to perform their duties impeccably to ensure security and cooperation with all parties, representing not only Qatar but also the Arab and Islamic worlds.During the meeting, the Minister reviewed the final preparations of the Qatari security force for the event, which fall under the administrative agreement on security cooperation between the State of Qatar and the French Republic. This participation underscores the expertise gained by Qatari security forces in organizing and securing major global events.He listened to detailed explanations from the leaders of the teams and specialized units regarding the specific tasks the force will undertake, according to the established plans and arrangements for the participation of the Qatari security forces in securing the event, in coordination with the organizing committee and related committees.The Qatari security forces will contribute to the security operations with teams representing specialized units such as the special unit (Lfdaweia), personal protection unit, tracking unit, explosives disposal unit, cybersecurity unit, security patrols, mounted patrols, anti-drone teams, planning teams, logistics teams, airport security teams, as well as the Command and Control Center and the International Police Cooperation Center (IPCC). This participation reflects the robust and growing strategic partnership between Qatar and France, particularly in security fields.The Qatari security forces completed a special training program for the Paris 2024 Olympics security mission, which lasted three weeks and included various training courses, workshops, and case studies of real events and situations from previous Olympic Games hosted in France and other countries, to derive valuable lessons.

Doha, Qatar: Minister of Interior and Commander of the Internal Security Force (Lekhwiya) HE Sheikh Khalifa bin Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani met with the Qatari security force set to participate in securing the Paris 2024 Olympic Games.

The Minister encouraged the force, comprised of leaders, experts, and security specialists, along with members of specialized teams and units, to utilize their skills and extensive experience to ensure the successful security of the event. He emphasized that Qatari national cadres have gained global recognition, especially following Qatar's outstanding success in hosting various international events to the highest security standards.



He conveyed greetings and directives from the Amir HH Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, and his directives that their mission in France is an extension of their responsibilities in Qatar, therefore, they have to perform their duties impeccably to ensure security and cooperation with all parties, representing not only Qatar but also the Arab and Islamic worlds.

During the meeting, the Minister reviewed the final preparations of the Qatari security force for the event, which fall under the administrative agreement on security cooperation between the State of Qatar and the French Republic. This participation underscores the expertise gained by Qatari security forces in organizing and securing major global events.



He listened to detailed explanations from the leaders of the teams and specialized units regarding the specific tasks the force will undertake, according to the established plans and arrangements for the participation of the Qatari security forces in securing the event, in coordination with the organizing committee and related committees.

The Qatari security forces will contribute to the security operations with teams representing specialized units such as the special unit (Lfdaweia), personal protection unit, tracking unit, explosives disposal unit, cybersecurity unit, security patrols, mounted patrols, anti-drone teams, planning teams, logistics teams, airport security teams, as well as the Command and Control Center and the International Police Cooperation Center (IPCC). This participation reflects the robust and growing strategic partnership between Qatar and France, particularly in security fields.



The Qatari security forces completed a special training program for the Paris 2024 Olympics security mission, which lasted three weeks and included various training courses, workshops, and case studies of real events and situations from previous Olympic Games hosted in France and other countries, to derive valuable lessons.
